title: Yanibar
content:

{{Quote|After many years of planning and preparations, I regret our diplomatic mission to Yanibar failed to convince the Zeison Sha of the Jedi Order's sincere hope that we might join forces.|Bodo Baas, the Tedryn Holocron|<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>}}
'''Yanibar''' was a [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] with a harsh environment that included seasonal climate extremes. At some point by [[3951 BBY]], it was [[Colonist/Legends|settled]] by a group of families and friends of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. The colony was intended to be a temporary refuge to help those people hide from attacks by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]; however, the Jedi who arranged the colonization were all [[Death/Legends|killed]] in combat and thus, Yanibar became forgotten. The colonists initially nearly succumbed to the planet's environmental challenges, but ultimately they survived due to the development of useful [[Force power/Legends|Force abilities]] among the population, and eventually the formation of the [[Zeison Sha]] tradition of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-users]], who acted as the colony's protectors and also developed an [[Anti-Jedi sentiment/Legends|anti-Jedi]] philosophy.

Yanibar was rediscovered by [[The fringe|fringe]] [[Merchant/Legends|traders]] after the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan|Battle of Ruusan]] of [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], and the planet became an outpost on the border of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] and Wild Space. The Jedi launched an unsuccessful [[Diplomatic mission to Yanibar|diplomatic mission]] to reconciliate with the Zeison Sha, but even after some of its members traveled offworld, the latter tradition remained resolutely independent from the Jedi and evaded the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Jedi-hunting]] attentions.

==Description==
{{Quote|Yanibar was a harsh world of seasonal extremes, and the original colonists might have perished had not some of them been Force-sensitive.|Tionne Solusar, ''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force''|<ref name="JvS" />}}
Yanibar was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Hero" /> located in the [[Yanibar system/Legends|Yanibar system]] of [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]],<ref name="TEA" /> close to the border with the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Hero" /><ref name="FDCR" /> It was a harsh world with dry, intensely [[Temperature/Legends|hot]] climate in [[Summer/Legends|summer]] and frigid, [[Water/Legends|wet]] [[Winter/Legends|winters]], and the occurrence of violent storms was also possible. The planet's environment was so extreme and unpredictable that most would consider it uninhabitable. Yanibar's fauna included the [[voorcat]], packs of which sometimes terrorized outlying regions on the world.<ref name="Hero" />

==History==
===Survival of a colony===
{{Quote|Almost two thousand years before the rise of the Empire, a group of families and friends of the Jedi fled to the remote Outer Rim planet Yanibar to escape the initial conflicts that led to the New Sith Wars.|Tionne Solusar, ''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force''|<ref name="JvS" />}}
Yanibar, at the time an out-of-the-way world, was [[Colonist/Legends|colonized]] by a group that consisted of families and friends of members of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] tradition of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-users]], with the former fleeing wars between the Jedi and their ideological anti-thesis, the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. The plan behind the settlement, organized by the Jedi, was for the group, which constituted potential targets for attacks by the Sith, to set up a temporary home on the planet until the Jedi could retrieve them once it was safe<ref name="Hero" /> due to the ongoing conflict having ended.<ref name="JATM" /> However, the Jedi in question were all eventually [[Death/Legends|killed]] in battle, leading to the colony on Yanibar becoming forgotten and isolated.<ref name="Hero" />

[[File:ZeisonSha.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Zeison Sha (a member pictured on the right) originated on Yanibar.]]
Initially, the colony struggled for survival, with thousands perishing<ref name="Hero" /> and the settlement losing most of its population within just the first few [[Standard year/Legends|years]].<ref name="JATM" /> As years passed, however, many of the colonists developed [[Force power/Legends|Force abilities]] that helped them adapt to the challenges posed by Yanibar's environment, leading to the emergence of the [[Zeison Sha]] tradition of [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-users. Over the subsequent [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]], the original reason behind the colonization of Yanibar was transformed into a myth of the local inhabitants. The Zeison Sha, who were not aware that the colony's original Jedi founders had been killed, began to view them as having abandoned the settlers. Over the span of generations, the Zeison Sha formulated a philosophy that rejected the Jedi as being uncaring and taking it upon themselves to serve as the protectors of Yanibar's community, which, over time, they helped to grow and prosper.<ref name="Hero" /> The Zeison Sha had become known offworld<ref name="KotOR2">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref> by [[3951 BBY]].<ref name="3951 BBY">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' to [[3951 BBY]].</ref>

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: The Old Republic: Shadow of Revan}}
Around [[3637 BBY]],<ref name="Date SoR">{{TORweb|url=community/showthread.php?p=9852711#edit9852711|text=Charles Boyd on Forums: "Dear Story Team, What Year Are We Currently In?" (1)}} — According to ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''{{'s}} lead designer, [[Charles Boyd]], the majority of the events of the digital expansion [[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Shadow of Revan|''Shadow of Revan'']] take place in approximately [[3637 BBY]]. The Crew Skill mission "Might of the Zeison Sha" is part of the ''Shadow of Revan'' expansion and therefore also takes place around that [[Standard year/Legends|year]].</ref> an individual affiliated with one of the sides in the ongoing [[Sith Wars/Legends|struggle]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]] sent a companion to Yanibar to carry out [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] ouvertures with the Zeison Sha, the support of which the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] faction represented by the two individuals had long aimed to obtain.<ref name="Might of the Zeison Sha">{{TORcite|exp=shadow|type=crewskill|Diplomacy|Might of the Zeison Sha}}</ref>
{{Endgame}}

===End of isolation===
{{Quote|A few centuries after the formation of the original colony, fringe traders visited Yanibar and were surprised to find a relatively thriving settlement.|Tionne Solusar, ''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force''|<ref name="JvS" />}}
After a few centuries, Yanibar's isolation lessened when it was discovered<ref name="Hero" /> by a passing [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] following the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan|Battle of Ruusan]]<ref name="CSWE Zeison Sha">{{CSWECite|III|369|Zeison Sha}}</ref> of [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]] that ended the [[New Sith Wars]] and—as was generally believed at the time—saw the elimination of the Sith.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> The planet was then sometimes visited by other [[The fringe|fringe]] traders,<ref name="Hero" /> who were surprised to discover a comparatively thriving colony there,<ref name="JvS" /> and the Zeison Sha eventually re-established contact with the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].<ref name="CSWE Zeison Sha" /> Yanibar subsequently came to serve as an outpost between known [[Realspace/Legends|space]] and the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]],<ref name="Hero" /> essentially being the last civilized stop for those traveling into Wild Space.<ref name="FDCR" /> Visits by offworlders were not frequent but became more so over several [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]].<ref name="Hero" />

[[File:Bodo_Baas_color.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Jedi Master Bodo Baas (hologram pictured) led a diplomatic mission to Yanibar.]]
Eventually, after the Zeison Sha learned of the conflicts against the Sith being over, some members of the tradition ventured offworld. However, many returned to their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] with indignant accounts of the Jedi practice of taking in Force-sensitive individuals<ref name="Hero" />—including the [[Youngling/Legends|children]] of some of the Zeison Sha themselves<ref name="CSWE Zeison Sha" />—for [[Jedi training/Legends|Jedi training]] at a young age. The Zeison Sha [[Anti-Jedi sentiment/Legends|anti-Jedi]] philosophy was thus further strengthened, and later Zeison Sha interstellar travelers avoided the Jedi.<ref name="Hero" /> For many years, the Jedi planned and prepared a [[diplomatic mission to Yanibar]] to convince the Zeison Sha to ally with them, and eventually [[Krevaaki]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Bodo Baas]] led the effort. The Zeison Sha reluctantly welcomed the visitors, though only on the condition that the Jedi would not attempt to meet any of their children without adult Zeison Sha being present, and allowed them to observe a Zeison Sha training session. Overall, however, Bodo Baas considered the mission a failure, and in [[580 BBY]], he recorded an account of the visit in the [[Tedryn Holocron]].<ref name="JvS" />

After the [[Declaration of a New Order|establishment]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="Hero" /> in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> the Zeison Sha traveling offworld avoided forces of the Imperial [[Jedi hunter/Legends|Jedi-hunting]]<ref name="Hero" /> [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]],<ref name="NEC" /> with members of Yanibar's Force-using tradition on the planet likewise escaping the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|purge]] of the Jedi.<ref name="CSWE Zeison Sha" /> Even by the time of the existence of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the Zeison Sha were putting in effort to avoid contact with the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi]], and, during<ref name="JATM" /> the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] by the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extragalactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]] between [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]] and [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]],<ref name="NEC" /> members of Yanibar's protector organization hoped the invaders would not discover their homeworld.<ref name="JATM" /> [[Jedi historian]] [[Tionne Solusar]] recorded Yanibar's history in her [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] compendium ''[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force]]''. Solusar's work, however,<ref name="JvS" /> erroneously<ref name="KotOR2" /><ref name="3951 BBY" /><ref name="Date SoR" /><ref name="Might of the Zeison Sha" /> claimed that the settlement of Yanibar that led to the formation of the Zeison Sha only occurred around two [[Millennium/Legends|thousand years]] before the establishment of the Empire, and that the discovery of the colony by fringe traders already took place "a few centuries" after its establishment.<ref name="JvS" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|The planet's inhabitants—a mix of humans and nonhumans including Duros, Twi'leks, and Rodians—maintained a relative measure of anonymity until the end of the New Sith Wars.|Tionne Solusar, ''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force''|<ref name="JvS" />}}
Yanibar was originally settled by a mixed group of beings which, during the early period of the colony's existence, numbered at least several thousands of people and among whom there was a significant presence of individuals sensitive to the Force. From them, the Zeison Sha tradition of Force-users eventually emerged, members of which served as the protectors of the settlement.<ref name="Hero" /> Over the span of mere decades,<ref name="JATM" /> with the influx of offworlders, Yanibar's small colony developed into a [[City/Legends|city]] with a sizeable population.<ref name="Hero" /> Originally,<ref name="JvS" /> however, the planetary community remained relatively obscure in the wider galaxy. The species makeup of<ref name="Hero" /> the original<ref name="JvS" /> Yanibar population included [[Duros/Legends|Duros]], [[Human/Legends|Humans]], [[Rodian/Legends|Rodians]], [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'leks]], and others, with the diversity of that community increasing after some of the colonists left the planet and revealed its existence to the wider [[galactic community]].<ref name="Hero" />

==Locations==
Yanibar hosted a city<ref name="Hero" /> and the [[Tho Yorla Spaceport]], the latter of which was frequented by crazy and desperate [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] and [[Scout/Legends|explorers]].<ref name="FDCR" /> The Zeison Sha also maintained on the planet the temple known as the [[Sha Kalan]].<ref name="JATM" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:HerosGuide.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Yanibar was introduced in ''Hero's Guide''.]]
Yanibar was first mentioned in ''[[Hero's Guide]]'', a [[2003]] [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' sourcebook written by [[J.D. Wiker]] and [[Rodney Thompson]].<ref name="Hero" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Yanibar [[Star system/Legends|system]], and thereby Yanibar itself, in grid square R-19.<ref name="TEA" />

''Hero's Guide'' states that Yanibar's colonization that led to the formation of the Zeison Sha took place "nearly two millennia before the rise of the Empire."<ref name="Hero" /> Although this general chronological placement was later corroborated by the [[2007]] reference book [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']],<ref name="JvS" /> [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="CSWE Zeison Sha" /> and the 2009 ''Star Wars Roleplaying Game'' sourcebook ''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]'',<ref name="JATM" /> it was also contradicted by the existence of the Zeison Sha by the time of the events of both the [[2004]] [[Obsidian Entertainment]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''<ref name="KotOR2" /> and [[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Shadow of Revan|''Shadow of Revan'']],<ref name="Might of the Zeison Sha" /> a [[2014]] expansion for [[BioWare]]'s [[2011]] video game ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'',<ref name="SWTOR dates">{{InsiderCite|207|The Stuff of Legends: A Decade of Star Wars: The Old Republic|The Stuff of Legends: A Decade of ''Star Wars'': The Old Republic}}</ref> which take place in 3951 [['ABY-BBY' dating system/Legends|BBY]]<ref name="3951 BBY" /> and around 3637 BBY, respectively,<ref name="Date SoR" /> or closer to ''four'' millennia before the rise of the Galactic Empire.<ref name="NEC" /> In addition, ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia''{{'s}} entry for Yanibar claims the planet was only the "likely" point of origin of the Zeison Sha,<ref name="CSWE Yanibar">{{CSWECite|III|349|Yanibar}}</ref> in contrast to the other ''[[Star Wars]]'' sources discussing the relationship between the sect and the planet<ref name="Hero" /><ref name="JATM" /><ref name="JvS" />—including the entry for the Zeison Sha in ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' itself.<ref name="CSWE Zeison Sha" />
